# Onboarding Checklist

A step-by-step guide for getting new team members up and running.

## Day 1

- [ ] Get access to the Git repo and clone locally
- [ ] Read the [[team-agreements]] page so you know how we work
- [ ] Install Go 1.22+ and Node.js 20+
- [ ] Run `folio doctor` and fix any flagged issues
- [ ] Introduce yourself in the team Slack channel

## First Week

- [ ] Pair with your onboarding buddy on a small bug fix
- [ ] Review the [[deployment-runbook]] to understand how releases work
- [ ] Read through [[adr-001-markdown-storage]] and [[adr-002-local-first]] to understand our key architectural decisions
- [ ] Set up your local dev environment following the README
- [ ] Attend the weekly planning meeting (see [[meeting-notes-2026-04-07]])

## First Month

- [ ] Complete at least one feature end-to-end
- [ ] Shadow an on-call rotation
- [ ] Write or update one wiki page based on something you learned
- [ ] Review the [[incident-retro-api-outage]] to see how we handle incidents

## Tips

Don't try to learn everything at once. The wiki is here so you can look things up as needed. If something is missing or unclear, **add it** — that's how the wiki stays useful.
